title: lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64 returns pointer to mutable static without const enforcement
description:
lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() returns 'const uint8_t *' to lora_state.eui64, but the caller could cast away const and mutate the EUI-64. More critically: the EUI-64 is read without synchronization with initialization.

Location:
- lora_l2.c:322-329 (lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64)
- lora_l2.h:129 (declaration)

What goes wrong: The initialized check at line 324 uses atomic_get(), but the subsequent return of the pointer creates a TOCTOU window. A concurrent lichen_lora_l2_init() call could be modifying eui64[] while the caller reads it. In practice the init is idempotent after first call, but the code pattern is fragile.

Additionally, if multiple threads call get_eui64() while one is in init(), they could see partially-written EUI-64 bytes (though this is unlikely given how init is typically called early).

Fix: Either document single-threaded init requirement, or return a copy of the EUI-64 into caller-provided buffer under the mutex, or keep current pattern but add memory barrier comment.
close_reason: Duplicate of .12 - same get_eui64 API issue
